description: Known sprite locations for various SNES ROMs
_note: Updated with sprite locations discovered through advanced ROM scanning on 2025-01-20

KIRBY SUPER STAR._status: Europe/Fun Pak sprites discovered via ROM scanner

High_Quality_Sprite_1.notes: Perfect quality score (1.00) - likely main character sprites

High_Quality_Sprite_2.notes: Very high quality (0.98) - smaller sprite set (112 tiles)

High_Quality_Sprite_3.notes: Excellent quality (0.96) - standard 8KB sprite block - aligned to tile boundary

Medium_Quality_Set_1.notes: Good quality (0.92) - smaller sprite set - aligned to tile boundary

Medium_Quality_Set_2.notes: Good quality (0.90) - alternate offset for sprite 3

Kirby_Main_Sprites.notes: CONFIRMED: Kirby character sprites - visually verified Dec 2025

Enemy_Sprites.notes: CONFIRMED: Enemy sprites with eyes - visually verified Dec 2025

Items_UI_Graphics.notes: CONFIRMED: Items and UI graphics - visually verified Dec 2025

Background_Tiles.notes: CONFIRMED: Background tiles (trees, terrain) - visually verified Dec 2025

Additional_Sprites.notes: CONFIRMED: Valid sprite data - visually verified Dec 2025

sprites._note: Offsets 0x180000-0x200000 are the primary sprite region. Visual verification is essential - quality scores alone cannot distinguish real sprites from noise.

offset_variants._note: Europe/Fun Pak ROM has different sprite locations than USA version

palettes.description: Kirby sprite palette data - discovered via kirby-color tool
palettes._note: Multiple copies exist throughout ROM at various offsets for different game states

king_dedede.description: King Dedede's palette - use for Dedede sprite replacements

scanning_hints.description: Tips for finding actual sprite data in ROMs
hints: IMPORTANT: Visual verification is essential - quality scores cannot distinguish real sprites from noise
Render with grayscale first to verify shapes, then apply palettes
The primary sprite region for Kirby is 0x180000-0x200000
Real sprite data should decompress to multiples of 32 bytes (8x8 tiles in 4bpp)
Character sprites typically show recognizable forms when converted to images
Kirby sprites should show round body shapes, faces, and character features
Quality scores above 0.80 may still be noise - always verify visually
Use palette 8 for Kirby (pink), palette 11 for enemies (green/orange)
